description_long: This house features a prominent hip roof with a red shingle finish and two skylights, giving it a modern yet rustic appearance. The main structure is clad in light-colored horizontal wooden siding, while an attached secondary volume is finished in a textured gray material with a matching red sloped roof. Large glass-paneled double doors dominate the front facade, allowing ample natural light into the interior. Windows are symmetrically placed, with a set of two on the roof and a single window on the side wall. The overall design is compact, with clean lines and a blend of traditional and contemporary elements. The house appears well-suited for a suburban or semi-urban neighborhood.
description: A compact house with a red hip roof, skylights, light wood siding, and large glass-paneled doors. It features a secondary attached volume with a sloped roof and textured gray exterior.
description_view_0: Front view showing large glass-paneled double doors, the main hip roof with skylights, and the attached secondary volume with a sloped roof.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the textured gray wall of the secondary volume and the two skylights on the main roof.
description_view_2: Rear angled view showing a single window on the wooden siding and the intersection of the two rooflines.
description_view_3: Back view displaying the full expanse of the hip roof, skylights, and the plain wooden siding with no visible doors or windows.
attribute_window_arrangement: symmetrical rows with large front glass doors and roof skylights
attribute_entryway_design: Large glass-paneled double doors with no visible porch or steps, framed by wooden siding and a modest overhang
